response: TEMPERATURE IS A MEASUREMENT OF THE HEAT ENERGY IN A SUBSTANCE. IT IS A KEY FACTOR IN MANY PHYSICAL AND CHEMICAL PROCESSES. THE TEMPERATURE OF A SOLUTION AFFECTS THE RATE OF REACTIONS. WHEN A SOLUTION IS HEATED, THE PARTICLES MOVE MORE FREELY. THIS CAN INCREASE THE RATE OF REACTIONS. IN BIOLOGY, TEMPERATURE IS IMPORTANT FOR ENZYMATIC REACTIONS. THE BODY REGULATES TEMPERATURE TO MAINTAIN A STABLE INTERNAL ENVIRONMENT. TEMPERATURE CAN AFFECT THE SOLUBILITY OF SOLIDS IN SOLUTIONS. WHEN SOLUTIONS ARE MIXED, TEMPERATURE CHANGES CAN OCCUR. THE RATE OF REACTION IS INFLUENCED BY TEMPERATURE. THE 12TH SENTENCE IS: "ROLLING A BALL ON A ROLLING ROLL IS A COMMON EXPERIMENT TO STUDY MOTION." THE LETTER S APPEARS 13 TIMES IN THIS RESPONSE.
